title: hydranthus scandens
intro: Hydranthus scandens, also known as climbing hydrangea, is a flowering vine native to China and Japan, characterized by its large white or purple flowers and ability to thrive in shaded areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Hydranthus scandens include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism. Herbal preparations of Hydranthus scandens are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create teas, or by using its roots to create tonics.
